headline: AI Models Nail SPY and QQQ Rally While Missing Energy Sector Chaos
summary: AI models showed strong consensus on SPY and QQQ's upward movement, with all five models correctly predicting the rally. However, predictions diverged wildly on individual stocks, with PLTR's massive 30% crash to $135.91 and XLE's stunning 33% plunge to $59.45 catching most models off-guard despite correct directional calls.

consensus_picks.note: All five models correctly predicted SPY's rise to $723.77, showing perfect consensus on the broader market rally.
Four models correctly anticipated QQQ's tech-driven surge to $681.61, with strong confidence levels.
Both models incorrectly predicted NVDA would bounce from oversold conditions, but it continued declining to $196.50.
Models disagreed on Tesla's direction, with Grok correctly calling the slight decline while others expected gains.

best_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence SPY call perfectly captured the market's recovery rally with the highest score of the day.

worst_call.summary: Grok's confident NVDA bounce prediction failed as the stock continued its decline despite positive AI sector sentiment.
